topic: AI — Artificial Intelligence: breakthroughs, regulation, business, trends

articles.title: Morgan Stanley warns an AI breakthrough is coming in 2026 — and most of the world isn't ready
articles.snippet: A major AI advancement is arriving in early 2026, according to Morgan Stanley's investment research. The bank projects transformative improvements in artificial intelligence capabilities, driven by massive compute accumulation at leading American AI research facilities. Researchers cited Elon Musk's perspective that increasing computational resources tenfold during language model training roughly doubles model intelligence, with existing scaling laws supporting this assertion. Recent progress already exceeds sector expectations: OpenAI's new GPT-5.4 'Thinking' model achieved 83.0% on the GDPVal benchmark, reaching expert-level performance on economically valuable tasks.

articles.title: Three charged in the US with smuggling AI chips into China
articles.snippet: Three individuals associated with artificial intelligence server manufacturer Super Micro Computer, including its cofounder, have been charged with helping smuggle at least $2.5 billion worth of United States AI technology to China in violation of export laws, according to the US Department of Justice. US prosecutors did not name Super Micro in the complaint, referring only to a 'US manufacturer,' but San Jose, California-based Super Micro said it was informed by federal prosecutors of the indictment on Thursday.

articles.title: President Donald J. Trump Unveils National AI Legislative Framework
articles.snippet: The Trump Administration is committed to winning the AI race to usher in a new era of human flourishing, economic competitiveness, and national security for the American people. Achieving these goals requires a commonsense national policy framework that both enables American industry to innovate and thrive and ensures that all Americans benefit from this technological revolution. The Administration recognizes that some Americans feel uncertain about how this transformative technology will affect issues they care about, like their children's wellbeing or their monthly electricity bill.

articles.title: How AI Is Driving Revenue, Cutting Costs and Boosting Productivity for Every Industry in 2026
articles.snippet: AI is everywhere and accelerating everything — becoming essential infrastructure to create the intelligence that will advance every industry. That's why companies are increasingly focusing on the technology's return on investment (ROI), as well as how to best apply AI to their own use cases. NVIDIA's annual 'State of AI' reports show how AI is being adopted across industries, what it's being used for and how companies are achieving ROI, as well as their challenges and goals with the technology.

articles.title: The biggest AI stories of the year (so far)
articles.snippet: The AI industry is constantly churning out news, like major acquisitions, indie developer successes, public outcry, and existentially dangerous contract negotiations. Key topics covered include NVIDIA, Meta, OpenAI developments, the rise of OpenClaw as a major open-source AI player, and emerging policy debates around AI governance.

articles.title: AI Update, March 20, 2026: AI News and Views From the Past Week
articles.snippet: Google signals future advertising opportunities within Gemini AI interface. Google has left open the possibility of introducing advertising into its Gemini AI assistant, signaling a major potential shift in digital advertising. As AI-generated answers reduce traditional search traffic, integrating ads into conversational interfaces could create new revenue streams. The move would require new ad formats embedded within AI responses and raises questions about user trust, transparency, and regulatory compliance.

articles.title: Palantir AI to support UK finance operations
articles.snippet: UK authorities believe improving efficiency across national finance operations requires applying AI platforms from vendors like Palantir. The country's financial regulator, the FCA, has initiated a project leveraging AI to identify illicit activities. The FCA is currently testing the Foundry platform from Miami-based software vendor Palantir. This three-month pilot costs upwards of £30,000 per week and focuses on mining the regulator's internal data lake to detect money laundering, insider trading, and fraud across the 42,000 financial services businesses under FCA's supervision.

articles.title: Visa prepares payment systems for AI agent-initiated transactions
articles.snippet: Payments rely on a simple model: a person decides to buy something, and a bank or card network processes the transaction. That model is starting to change as Visa tests how AI agents can initiate payments. Visa is rolling out its 'Agentic Ready' programme in Europe to test how financial systems handle AI-initiated transactions. The effort involves collaboration with banks, including Commerzbank and DZ Bank, preparing existing payment infrastructure for a scenario where software agents can search for products, make decisions, and complete purchases on behalf of users.

articles.title: DDN, Supermicro, and NVIDIA Launch Driving AI Breakthroughs Experience at GTC 2026
articles.snippet: DDN, the world's leading NVIDIA AI data platform provider and Supermicro Computer, Inc. (SMCI), and NVIDIA, powering advanced AI, today announced Driving AI Breakthroughs, a joint AI Factory initiative debuting at NVIDIA GTC 2026. Delivered through a custom-built mobile NVIDIA-powered AI factory, this immersive experience gives enterprise leaders a hands-on blueprint for architecting AI systems engineered for efficiency, utilization, and return on investment.

articles.title: До государственной критической инфраструктуры допустят лишь российский ИИ
articles.snippet: Государственные информационные системы и объекты критической инфраструктуры смогут применять только тот искусственный интеллект, при использовании которого обработка данных ведется исключительно на территории России, следует из законопроекта об ИИ, опубликованного ранее в среду для общественного обсуждения. Законопроект предполагает, что в государственных информационных системах и на значимых объектах критической информационной инфраструктуры можно будет применять только модели искусственного интеллекта, включенные в реестр доверенных моделей ИИ.

articles.title: Я тебя породил, я тебя и приручу. Попытка подчинить искусственный интеллект естественному
articles.snippet: Технологии искусственного интеллекта начали проникать в нашу жизнь более 10 лет назад, но стремительные обороты набрали только в последний год. Ранее это ограничивалось демонстрационными слайдами, робкими экспериментами с невнятными результатами и антиутопиями о бунте машин. Но мы уже наблюдаем первые побочные эффекты от внедренных технологий, и задача не дать искусственному интеллекту выйти из-под контроля интеллекта естественного приобрела критическую важность.

articles.title: Главное из мира ИИ за неделю (с 1 по 7 марта 2026 года)
articles.snippet: Все важные новости и тексты про искусственный интеллект за неделю с 1 по 7 марта 2026 года. Нейросеть деанонимизирует пользователей Reddit с точностью 90%: конец приватности в сети? Исследователи из ETH Zurich и Anthropic доказали, что анонимности в интернете не существует. ИИ научился читать старые комментарии на форумах (жалобы на аллергию, вопросы по коду, породу собаки) и сопоставлять их с реальными профилями людей в LinkedIn.

articles.title: 9 событий в мире ИИ, которые завирусились в соцсетях (24 февраля — 9 марта 2026)
articles.snippet: В США зреет масштабный социальный бунт против искусственного интеллекта. Только за один квартал местные жители заблокировали возведение дата-центров почти на сто миллиардов долларов. Nippon Life Insurance подала иск против OpenAI. Американка Анжела Липпс из-за ошибки системы распознавания лиц с ИИ безвинно провела полгода в тюрьме и вышла на свободу лишь в середине марта 2026 года.

articles.title: OpenClaw's ChatGPT moment sparks concern that AI models are becoming commodities
articles.snippet: OpenClaw, an open-source AI startup, has triggered a major industry debate after releasing a model that matches or exceeds proprietary alternatives at a fraction of the cost. The development raises concerns that AI models are becoming commodities, threatening the business models of companies that have invested billions in building proprietary systems. Industry analysts warn this could reshape the competitive landscape of the AI sector.

articles.title: Mastercard keeps tabs on fraud with new foundation model
articles.snippet: Mastercard launched a custom foundation model to strengthen fraud detection and monitoring capabilities across payment systems. The new AI model represents a significant advancement in financial security, leveraging Mastercard's vast transaction data to identify fraudulent patterns in real-time across global payment networks.
